preregistration.statement: This fixture file is the PRE-REGISTRATION for VERDICT 042. It was authored BEFORE the runner (mining_economy_sim.py) was written or executed; the results reference it. Commit order in this branch shows fixtures+engine before results.
preregistration.reserved_siblings: VERDICT 040 (ship-it bundle) and VERDICT 041 (narrow-TAM cookbooks) are reserved sibling numbers per the coordinator dispatch; this slice never renumbers down.

binding_spec.order: idea-engine control/inbox.md @ 8218d6630f53633461d993d9a3caa4ad54ab251d, ORDER 006 (2026-07-13T09:02Z), SUPERBOT-GAMES BALANCE item 4
binding_spec.order_verbatim: (4) MINING-ECONOMY-TUNING — (a) surface descend-gate shape: a fresh player with only an iron pickaxe has max_accessible_depth==0 and cannot descend until a torch (depth_access=1) / lantern (=2) is equipped (games/mining/core/world.py + equipment.py); (b) faucet/sink gap: base roll 1–2 ore/dig at 1–3 coin values vs iron sword 60 coins (market.GEAR_SHOP) and Forge I 3,000 coins + 25 iron + 15 stone / Forge II 8,000 + 20 gold + 10 iron (structures._FORGE_BUILD_LADDER);
binding_spec.packet: menno420/superbot-games @ 57f69be34785afb427d608b207e7369025166e94, control/outbox.md § 'SIM-REQUEST · mining-economy-tuning · 2026-07-13' (status: open). The packet carries an inline self-correction on the Forge ladder ('a Forge I at 8 000 coins + 20 gold + 10 iron … correction: 3 000 coins + 25 iron + 15 stone for Forge I, 8 000 coins + 20 gold + 10 iron for Forge II') — the corrected values are the pinned ones and match ORDER 006 item 4.
binding_spec.packet_faucet_estimate: the packet estimates 'on the order of ~22 coins per 8 digs' — a REPORTING-ONLY leg re-derives this figure and checks it against the exact faucet; it is a soft estimate, not a B0 anchor, and cannot flip the decision

b0_validity_anchors.note: Every anchor is driven through the byte-copied engine (engine/games/mining/core/*, sha256 MANIFEST.json @ pin 57f69be3). ANY mismatch = validity failure -> honest NULL, no probe result counts.

faucet.order_006_phrase: 1–3 coin values (stone 1 / bronze 2 / iron 3); the packet's own comment additionally pins silver 4 / gold 6 / diamond 12 which are checked as auxiliary anchors

auxiliary_engine_pins.note: engine constants the probes READ live from the copied modules (not packet anchors; listed so drift is visible)

energy.provenance: energy.py's own docstring: 'sized to the sim-pinned ~360-digs-per-active-hour sustained throttle'

action_time_model.burst: first MAX_ENERGY = 60 digs from a full bar at the interaction floor
action_time_model.INT-1: interaction floor = 2 s per action (registered assumption; UI/latency, not an engine constant — flip axis: a slower floor scales burst times linearly, sustained times are engine-pinned)
action_time_model.INT-2: non-dig actions (chop / craft / equip / sell / buy / descend) cost 0 energy and 1 interaction each in the pure core (the workflow layer may add costs; flip axis named)

P0_fresh_floor.cells: NORMAL only (conservative floor — no grid bonus)

registered_profiles.NP1_natural_progression: composite: start P0 -> dig to 10 coins, buy torch, descend (P2) -> dig to +40 coins, buy lantern, descend (P3) -> buy iron sword at +60 -> Forge I (3000 coins + 25 iron + 15 stone) -> Forge II (+8000 coins + 20 gold + 10 iron); fluid expectation accounting on exact per-dig means, materials tracked to check whether coins or materials bind
registered_profiles.GRID-1: roaming is modeled as i.i.d. cell-feature draws at engine weights; validated by an engine-exact MC leg sampling grid.cell_at over random coordinates (deterministic per (seed,x,y,z)) — if observed feature frequencies disagree with _FEATURE_WEIGHTS beyond MC tolerance, GRID-1 is marked broken and the NORMAL-only floor rules

probe_A_descend_gate_arms.G0_current: light-gated (the shipped shape). Two fresh-player paths measured: CHOP-CRAFT (chop randint(1,3) wood/action without axe -> craft torch at recipe {wood:2} -> equip -> descend; exact distribution) and DIG-SELL-BUY (dig at P0 -> sell -> buy torch at 10 coins -> equip -> descend; exact DP over the per-dig coin pmf for E[digs] and quantiles)
probe_A_descend_gate_arms.G1_pickaxe_grants_depth_1: counterfactual: any pickaxe carries depth_access=1 -> first descend = 1 action; consequences priced (light slot loses its Cavern gate; lantern still gates Deep)
probe_A_descend_gate_arms.G2_torch_cheaper_earlier: counterfactual: torch at 5 coins / recipe {wood:1} -> both paths recomputed
probe_A_descend_gate_arms.G3_unchanged: ratify arm (identical to G0; present so the ruling names it explicitly)

probe_B_faucet_sink_measures: exact E[coins/dig], E[stone/dig], E[iron/dig], E[gold/dig] per profile (Fraction arithmetic, engine constants read live)
coins/hour sustained (x360) and burst pacing per profile
digs-to-iron-sword (60 coins) at P0 and P1
NP-1 cumulative digs and wall-clock (sustained model) to torch, lantern, iron sword, Forge I, Forge II; binding-resource check (coins vs iron/stone/gold)

FAUCET-BYPASS reporting leg: rations/energy drinks convert coins->energy at 20/25 and 40/50 coins-per-dig equivalents; price the booster-fueled active path (digs at INT-1 pace, net coins/dig after booster cost) and its collapsed times-to-Forge — REPORTING + flag, cannot flip the primary ruling (primary = the engine's own sustained throttle)

TOOL-LADDER reporting leg: E[amount/dig] for bare hands / pickaxe / iron pickaxe / gold pickaxe / diamond pickaxe under BASE_ROLL_MAX=2 and round() — checks the rewards.py docstring claim that 'a better tool still pays' at every tier (closed-form prediction: pickaxe and iron pickaxe are amount-identical to bare hands on the {1,2} support; gold/diamond pay). REPORTING + candidate-row material, cannot flip the primary ruling

PACKET-ARITHMETIC reporting leg: reconstruct the packet's '~22 coins per 8 digs' (prediction: it is the stone/bronze/iron-only value average 28/15 x 1.5 amount = 2.8 coins/dig ~ 22.4/8digs, i.e. it ignores silver/gold/diamond; exact faucet is ~1.6-1.7x higher)

acceptance_bands.note: registered judgment lines in DIGS/ACTIONS (the engine's native units — minutes depend only on the engine-pinned 360/hr map and INT-1); full tables ship so a re-drawn line re-reads, never re-runs

BAND-GATE.ratify_if: worst-case DETERMINISTIC actions to first descend under G0 <= 20 AND <= 2.0 min at INT-1 pacing

BAND-FORGE1.ratify_if: NP-1 cumulative digs to Forge I in [200, 2200] (~33 min – 6.1 h sustained)

BAND-FORGE2.ratify_if: NP-1 cumulative digs to Forge II in [500, 4400] (~1.4 – 12.2 h sustained)

decision_rule.NULL: if ANY b0_validity_anchor mismatches -> validity failure -> verdict NULL (needs-more-evidence), the mismatch IS the finding; no probe result counts
decision_rule.ask_a: RATIFY current gate iff BAND-GATE ratify condition holds for G0; else recommend the cheapest arm (G1/G2) whose worst-case passes, with exact numbers
decision_rule.ask_b: RATIFY current faucet/sink constants iff BAND-SWORD, BAND-FORGE1 and BAND-FORGE2 all land in their ratify ranges; if a band fails, recommend the smallest single-constant change (computed exactly) that re-enters it: BAND-SWORD -> GEAR_SHOP['iron sword']; BAND-FORGE1 -> _FORGE_BUILD_LADDER[0].coins; BAND-FORGE2 -> _FORGE_BUILD_LADDER[1].coins
decision_rule.overall: approve (= ratify current constants, both asks) if ask_a and ask_b both ratify; conditional if exactly one ask needs a change; reject if both need changes; reporting legs (FAUCET-BYPASS, TOOL-LADDER, PACKET-ARITHMETIC) are flags/candidate rows and CANNOT flip the ruling

closed_form_predictions_pre_run.note: registered before the runner exists (V038 discipline); the run must confirm or the discrepancy is reported
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.E_amount_NORMAL_iron_pickaxe: 3/2 (round(1*1.25)=1, round(2*1.25)=2 — banker's rounding makes the x1.25 amount-inert on the {1,2} support)
closed_form_predictions_pre_run.gate_G0_chop_path: E[chops]=4/3, worst-case deterministic 2 chops -> 5 actions total (chop,chop,craft,equip,descend); E[actions]=4/3+3=13/3~4.33

rng_policy.primary: ZERO decision-bearing RNG — every ruling number is exact Fraction arithmetic on engine constants read live from the byte-copied modules
rng_policy.mc_validation_legs: seeded engine-driven Monte Carlo confirms the exact numbers within tolerance (self-check, not decision-bearing): seeds [20260792, 20260793, 20260794, 20260795] (strictly above the fleet high-water 20260791), N=200000 digs/profile, agreement gate |MC-exact|/exact <= 0.01; plus the GRID-1 engine-exact leg sampling grid.cell_at over 200000 random coords
rng_policy.reproducibility: one command, stdlib-only; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two full process runs (external diff)
